key_assumptions.source: Sum of parts / catalyst path in IRR steps
Model choice in valuation.json (not company guidance)

key_assumptions.input: Residual to Lawrence base payoff uplift

open_questions: Confirm fully diluted share count after PIPE, Stage 2 (**14.5M** shares), and warrant exercises post June 30, 2025
GEM arbitration outcome and cash/share settlement timing
Tanbreez DFS publication and Greenland Stage 2 approval status
Whether BMW **$15M** advance should enter risked floor when restrictions ease
Component proof judgments (Tanbreez success probability and gross success value) vs next filing milestones
thesis: Critical Metals Corp. is a Nasdaq-listed mining exploration and development company focused on critical minerals for electrification and Western supply-chain security. **[Fact]** Its flagship asset is the Tanbreez rare earth project in southern Greenland, where the company holds a **42%** equity interest in Tanbreez Mining Greenland A/S with a contracted path to **92.5%** subject to Greenland government approval. **[Fact]** The company also owns the Wolfsberg lithium project in Austria (BMW offtake and **$15 million** advance payment) and minority stakes in Weinebene and Eastern Alps lithium projects. **[Fact]** FY2025 net loss was **$51.9 million** on **104.9 million** ordinary shares; cash was **$7.3 million** at June 30, 2025 with a going-concern warning. **[Fact]** The company has declared mineral resources but has not declared reserves or begun production.
why_market_wrong: **Predictive attribute:** complexity discount (Greenland jurisdiction, pre-reserve resource, related-party Tanbreez history, and headline PEA net present value versus thin cash floor).
